content_standard: B3.1.6.1 Exhibit an understanding of appropriate non-formal forms of greeting and communication.
indicator_description: Say why we have non-verbal greetings.
examplars: 1. Discuss with learners why we use non-verbal forms to greet.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Talking about Oneself, Family, People and Places
content_standard: B3.1.7.1 Apply the use of pronouns appropriately in simple sentences
indicator_description: Describe one’s self using personal pronouns.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Write some words on the board and lead learners to say the words.
3. Let learners form sentences with the words.
4. Introduce pronoun to learners.
5. Let learners describe themselves using personal pronouns. E.g. I, me, mine.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Talking about Oneself, Family, People and Places
content_standard: B3.1.7.1 Apply the use of pronouns appropriately in simple sentences
indicator_description: Describe the family using the appropriate pronouns
examplars:
1. Use pronouns to talk about a family.
2. Let learners describe their families using the appropriate pronouns.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.1.8.1 Show an understanding of answering questions from a passage.
indicator_description: Recount what the story is about and answer simple questions on the story.
examplars:
1. Lead learners to sing a song.
2. Tell/show an interesting story.
3. Discuss the story with learners.
4. Lead learners to discuss what the story is about.
5. Ask questions based on the story for learners to answer.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.1.8.1 Show an understanding of answering questions from a passage.
indicator_description: Explain the meaning of key words and give simple summary of a short text.
examplars:
1. Lead learners to sing a song.
2. Read a story.
3. Let learners read the story as a group and in turns.
4. Write the key words in the story on the board.
5. Use keywords from the story in different contexts.
6. Let learners also use the key words to form sentences.
7. Allow learners to give contextual meaning of some key words.
8. Give a summary of the text read.
9. Let learners read the text again.
10. Group learners and encourage them to write a simple summary of the short text.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.1.9.1 Demonstrate knowledge on understanding and answering of questions correctly by the use of question words.
indicator_description: Answer questions on, ‘who”, “what”, “where” and” when”
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Write the question words on flashcards.
3. Lead learners to read the question words.
4. Use the question words to form questions and lead learners to read.
5. Use the flashcards to assist learners to recognise when to use question words. “what”, “who”,” where”,” when”.
6. Let learners form their own questions using the question words discussed.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.1.9.1 Demonstrate knowledge on understanding and answering of questions correctly by the use of question words.
indicator_description: Recognise and tell when to use the question words “how” and “why”
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Write the question words on flashcards.
3. Lead learners to read the question words.
4. Use the question words to form questions and lead learners to read.
5. Use the flashcards to assist learners to recognise when to use question words “how” and “why”.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.1.9.1 Demonstrate knowledge on understanding and answering of questions correctly by the use of question words.
indicator_description: Answer and ask ‘who”, “what”, “where”, “when”, “how” and “why” questions.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Write the question words on flashcards.
3. Lead learners to read the question words.
4. Use the question words to form questions and lead learners to read.
5. Use the flashcards to assist learners to recognise when to use question words.
6. Write questions using the question words ‘what’, ‘when and ‘how’ and ‘why’ on the board.
7. Lead learners to read the questions.
8. Let learners read the questions in turns.
9. Help learners to recognise when to use question word “why”.
10. Let learners form their own questions using the question word “why.”

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.1.10.1 Exhibit knowledge of understanding and listening attentively to simple instructions and directions and carrying them out.
indicator_description: Understand instructions and carry them out.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Instruct some learners to issue an instruction for the rest to carry out the instruction.
3. Let learners discuss and understand instructions and carry them out.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.1.10.1 Exhibit knowledge of understanding and listening attentively to simple instructions and directions and carrying them out.
indicator_description: Understand directions and carry them out.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Direct some learners to a given place in the school.
3. Let learners discuss directions, understand and follow directions.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.1.10.1 Exhibit knowledge of understanding and listening attentively to simple instructions and directions and carrying them out.
indicator_description: Give directions accurately.
examplars:
1. Direct some learners to a given place in the school.
2. Help learners to give directions accurately to that place.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.1.11.1 Demonstrate knowledge on the days of the week and say the time by the hour and minutes.
indicator_description: Say the time by the hour and half hour.
examplars:
1. Show a clock to learners.
2. Discuss the use of a clock with learners.
3. Introduce the hour and minute hands to learners.
4. Discuss the functions of the hour and minutes hands with learners.
5. Help learners to tell time by the hour and half hour. E.g. The time is 9 o’clock. The time is 9:30

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.1.11.1 Demonstrate knowledge on the days of the week and say the time by the hour and minutes.
indicator_description: Say the time by the hour and minutes.
examplars:
1. Show a clock to learners and discuss its function with them.
2. Revise the functions of the hour and minutes hands with learners.
3. Help learners to tell time by the hour and minute. E.g. The time is 9:30. The time is 9:15.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.1.11.1 Demonstrate knowledge on the days of the week and say the time by the hour and minutes.
indicator_description: Say the days of the week and names associated with the days.
examplars:
1. Lead learners to explore a rhyme with gestures.
2. Write the names of days of the week on flashcards.
3. Lead learners to read the names of the days of the week.
4. Use the flashcards to help learners to recognise names associated with the days of the week. E.g. Monday, Tuesday, etc.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge of listening, recognising and differentiating sounds in spoken words.
indicator_description: Recognise and separate syllables.
examplars:
1. Lead learners to say the letters of the alphabet as a group.
2. Call learners to say the letters of the alphabet in turns.
3. Practice syllable recognition and separation with learners on the board.
4. Allow learners to apply the knowledge to recognise and separate syllables in words.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge of listening, recognising and differentiating sounds in spoken words.
indicator_description: Add individual sounds in simple one-syllable words to make up new words.
examplars:
1. Lead learners to say the letters of the alphabet as a group.
2. Call learners to say the letters of the alphabet in turns.
3. Practice adding individual sounds to one syllabic words.
4. Encourage learners to add sounds to one-syllabic words to form new words on their own. E.g. /ba/ + /g/ = bag /ba/ + /t/ = bat

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge of listening, recognising and differentiating sounds in spoken words.
indicator_description: Substitute one sound in two-syllable words to make up new words.
examplars:
1. Lead learners to say the letters of the alphabet as a group.
2. Call learners to say the letters of the alphabet in turns.
3. Demonstrate adding one sound to two-syllabic words to form new words on the board.
4. Let learners apply the knowledge to substitute one sound in two syllable words to make up new words. E.g. /table/ -/t/ + /c/ = /cable/ /carrot/ - /c/ + /p/ = /parrot/ /toffee/ -/t/ + /c/=/coffee/

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.4.1 Show an understanding of connecting sounds to letters and blending letters into syllables in order to read.
indicator_description: Use alphabetic awareness to decode words.
examplars:
1. Lead learners to say the letters of the alphabet as a group.
2. Call learners to say the letters of the alphabet in turns.
3. Practice alphabetic awareness to decode words on the board for learners to observe.
4. Aid learners to use alphabetic awareness to decode simple words.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.4.1 Show an understanding of connecting sounds to letters and blending letters into syllables in order to read.
indicator_description: Use alphabetic awareness to decode compound words.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet as a group and individually.
2. Practice alphabetic awareness with learners to decode compound words on the board.
3. Assist learners to use alphabetic awareness to decode compound words.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.4.1 Show an understanding of connecting sounds to letters and blending letters into syllables in order to read.
indicator_description: Read simple short sentences that include compound word.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Write and read simple short sentences that include compound words on the board.
3. Lead learners to read the sentences aloud.
4. Underline the compound words in the sentences and encourage learners to use the words to form simple short sentences that include compound words.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.4.2 Recognise and read words with identical sounds.
indicator_description: Listen and say words with identical sounds from poems.
examplars:
1. Let learners explore a rhyme they know.
2. Read poems to learners.
3. Ask learners to listen attentively and pick words with identical sounds.
4. Let learners say the words with identical sounds from the poem.
5. Let learners use the words to form their own sentences.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.4.2 Recognise and read words with identical sounds.
indicator_description: Read poems and recognise rhyming words.
examplars:
1. Let learners explore a rhyme they know.
2. Read or play a poem to learners.
3. Lead learners through discussions to recognise the rhyming words in the poem.
4. Ask learners to explore the poem and recognise rhyming words from the poem.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.4.2 Recognise and read words with identical sounds.
indicator_description: Read two-syllable words.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Form one-syllable words and lead learners to say the words.
3. Join two of the one-syllable words to form a new word on the board.
4. Lead learners to say the words.
5. Assist learners to read two syllable words.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.4.2 Recognise and read words with identical sounds.
indicator_description: Read three-syllable words and segment them into syllables.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Form one-syllable words and lead learners to say the words.
3. Join two of the one-syllable words to form a new word on the board.
4. Lead learners to say the words.
5. Write three-syllable words on flashcards.
6. Show it to the learners to read the words and segment them into syllables.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.4.2 Recognise and read words with identical sounds.
indicator_description: Read one-syllable words with consonant clusters.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Form one-syllable words and lead learners to say the words.
3. Join two of the one-syllable words to form a new word on the board.
4. Read several one-syllable words with consonant clusters to learners.
5. Allow learners to read one syllable words with consonant clusters.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.5.1 Show an understanding of recognising and reading about things in their environment.
indicator_description: Read texts aloud with correct pronunciation and tone.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet as a group and then individually.
2. With correct pronunciation and tone read texts aloud.
3. Let learners read texts aloud with correct pronunciation and tone as a group and then individually.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.5.1 Show an understanding of recognising and reading about things in their environment.
indicator_description: Play a variety of words and sentence games.
examplars:
1. Revise the lesson on traditional games with learners.
2. Put learners into groups.
3. Let them play a word and sentence game.
4. Allow learners to play with a variety of words and sentence games.
5. Let learners talk about their experiences group by group.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.6.1 Exhibit knowledge of answering questions based on texts presented.
indicator_description: Answer questions based on passage read to them.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet as a group and then individually.
2. With correct pronunciation and tone read texts aloud.
3. Let learners read the text in turns.
4. Ask questions based on the text read for learners to answer.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.6.1 Exhibit knowledge of answering questions based on texts presented.
indicator_description: Answer questions based on passage read by learners.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet as a group and then individually.
2. Give textbooks to learners.
3. Lead learners to read the text on a given page as a group and individually.
4. Ask questions based on the text they have read for them to answer.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.7.1 Demonstrate knowledge on reading for comprehension
indicator_description: Read longer sentences.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet as a group and then individually.
2. Write long sentences on a card.
3. Show the card with the sentences to learners.
4. Read aloud the long sentences.
5. Let learners read longer sentences as a group and the individually

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.7.1 Demonstrate knowledge on reading for comprehension
indicator_description: Read short passages/text.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet as a group and then individually.
2. Write a short passage on a card.
3. Show the card to learners.
4. Read aloud the long passage.
5. Let learners read the short passage as a group and the individually.
6. Ask learners questions based on the short passage read for them to answer.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.2.8.1 Exhibit knowledge by reading aloud with minimal mistakes.
indicator_description: Read short passages of about six to ten sentences aloud using correct tone.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet as a group and then individually.
2. Write a short passage of about six to ten sentences.
3. Read aloud the short passage of about six to ten sentences using correct tone for learners to listen.
4. Lead learners to read the passage.
5. Let learners read aloud the passage as a group and then individually.
6. Ask questions based on the passage read for learners to answer.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.3.1.1 Write and practise saying letters or words with which learners have difficulty; copy various sentences, verses and words of songs legibly.
indicator_description: Write letters and words.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet as a group and individually.
2. Write letter and words boldly on the board.
3. Give learners some letters and words to write on their own.
4. Assist learners to write letters and words given them.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.3.1.1 Write and practise saying letters or words with which learners have difficulty; copy various sentences, verses and words of songs legibly.
indicator_description: Copy simple sentences boldly and clearly.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet as a group and individually.
2. Write simple sentences boldly on the board.
3. Lead learners to read the sentences aloud as a group.
4. Let learners read the sentences aloud in turns.
5. Let learners copy the simple sentences boldly and clearly in their books.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Writing Simple Words/Names of People and Places (Proper Nouns) Labelling Items in the Environment/Classroom
content_standard: B3.3.3.1 Write simple words/names of people and places (proper nouns).
indicator_description: Write simple words/names of people and places.
examplars:
1. Let learners mention names of places and names of their friends.
2. Create people and place names map on a cardboard.
3. Show the map to learners and discuss it with them.
4. Assist learners to write simple words or names of people and places in their books.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Writing Simple Words/Names of People and Places (Proper Nouns) Labelling Items in the Environment/Classroom
content_standard: B3.3.3.2: Show understanding of items in the environment/ classroom.
indicator_description: Say and label items in the classroom and in the environment.
examplars:
1. Ask learners to sing a popular song they know.
2. Display some items in the environment in the classroom.
3. Call a learner to lead the class to discuss, recognise and name the items.
4. Draw the items on the board and write their names on a card.
5. Call learners to pick a card and fix it under the item the name written on the card refers to.
6. Mention some items seen in the classroom and ask learners to point at the items mentioned.
7. Let learners mention and label more items in the classroom and in the environment.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Copying/Writing Simple Sentences with Correct Spacing
content_standard: B3.3.4.1 Exhibit knowledge of writing simple sentences with correct word spacing.
indicator_description: Write correct sentences with correct spacing from substitution table.
examplars:
1. Let learners explore a rhyme they know.
2. Make a substitution table on the board.
3. Lead learners to say aloud the words in the substitution table.
4. Let learners say aloud the words in the substitution table individually.
5. Demonstrate how sentences are formed from a substitution table.
6. Help learners to write correct sentences with correct spacing from the substitution table.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Copying/Writing Simple Sentences with Correct Spacing
content_standard: B3.3.4.1 Exhibit knowledge of writing simple sentences with correct word spacing.
indicator_description: Use upper case or lower-case letters correctly in sentences with correct spacing.
examplars:
1. Let learners explore a rhyme they know or say the letters of the alphabet as a group.
2. Create upper and lower-case letters game.
3. Play the game with learners.
4. Let one learner lead the other learners to play the game (supervision is necessary).
5. Write sentences on the board using upper and lower-case letters.
6. Help learners to use upper case or lower-case letters correctly in writing sentences with correct spacing.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.5.1.1 Exhibit knowledge of using capital letters appropriately.
indicator_description: Understand the use of capital letters to write proper nouns.
examplars:
1. Let learners sing any play song they know.
2. Create a game that helps to use capital letters to write proper nouns.
3. Through the game help learners to understand the use of capital letters to write proper nouns.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.5.1.1 Exhibit knowledge of using capital letters appropriately.
indicator_description: Comprehend the use of capital letters to write sentences.
examplars:
1. Let learners sing any play song they know.
2. Create a game that helps learners to use capital letters to write sentences.
3. Help learners to understand the use of capital letters to write correct sentences.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.5.1.1 Exhibit knowledge of using capital letters appropriately.
indicator_description: Understand and use capital letters after sentences and full stops.
examplars:
1. Let a learner lead the class to sing a popular song they know.
2. Tell learners why we use capital letters after sentences and full stops.
3. Help learners to understand the use of capital letters after sentences and full stops using illustrations on the board.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.5.2.1 Show an understanding of using punctuation marks appropriately
indicator_description: Understand and use full stops and commas.
examplars:
1. Let a learner lead the class to sing a popular song they know.
2. Create a game on the Show an understanding of the use of full stops and commas.
3. Play the game with learners.
4. Help learners to understand the use of full stops and commas.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.5.2.1 Show an understanding of using punctuation marks appropriately
indicator_description: Understand the use of full stops at the end of sentences.
examplars:
1. Let the class sing a popular traditional occupational song they know.
2. Create a game on the use of full stops.
3. Play the game with learners and let learners play the game in groups.
4. Help learners to show an understanding of the use of full stops at the end of sentences.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.5.2.1 Show an understanding of using punctuation marks appropriately
indicator_description: Recognise and use punctuation marks in sentences.
examplars:
1. Let the class sing a popular traditional occupational song they know.
2. Create a game on the use of punctuation marks.
3. Play the game with learners and let learners play the game in groups.
4. Help learners to recognise and use punctuation marks in sentences.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Integrating Grammar in Written Language (Use of Action Words)
content_standard: B3.5.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge on recognition of action words.
indicator_description: Recognise present continuous action words in sentences
examplars:
1. Write sentences in continuous action on cardboards.
2. Discuss it with learners.
3. Help learners to recognise present continuous action words in sentences. E.g. going, coming, eating and drinking.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Integrating Grammar in Written Language (Use of Action Words)
content_standard: B3.5.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge on recognition of action words.
indicator_description: Write present continuous action words correctly.
examplars:
1. Write present continuous action words on flashcards.
2. Lead learners to say the words as a group and then individually.
3. Use the flashcards to help learners to write present continuous action words in sentences.
4. Give learners some action words and let learners write the present continuous forms. E.g. Sitting, walking, jumping and thinking.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Integrating Grammar in Written Language (Use of Action Words)
content_standard: B3.5.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge on recognition of action words.
indicator_description: Use present continuous action words in simple sentences.
examplars:
1. Let learners sing a popular play song they know.
2. Through games help learners to use present continuous action words in sentences. E.g. I am sitting on a chair. I am walking home. Kojo is jumping.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Integrating Grammar in Written Language (Use of Qualifying Words)
content_standard: B3.5.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge on recognition of qualifying words (adjectives and adverbs)
indicator_description: Know and use comparative words/adjectives in sentences.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the names of the days of the week.
2. Use simple adjectives to form simple sentences and lead learners to read.
3. Underline the adjectives and discuss the adjectives with learners.
4. Help learners to understand and use adjectives to form simple sentences. E.g. Kofi is taller than Yaw. Ama is smaller than Adwoa.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Integrating Grammar in Written Language (Use of Qualifying Words)
content_standard: B3.5.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge on recognition of qualifying words (adjectives and adverbs)
indicator_description: Demonstrate knowledge of the use of adverbs.
examplars:
1. Revise the lesson on command and response with learners.
2. Write some action words on a card and lead learners to say the words aloud.
3. Discuss the action words with learners.
4. Use the action words to form sentences (the sentences should have adverbs in them).
5. Through that help learners to understand adverbs and how they are used.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Integrating Grammar in Written Language (Use of Qualifying Words)
content_standard: B3.5.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge on recognition of qualifying words (adjectives and adverbs)
indicator_description: Recognise adverbs from a group of words.
examplars:
1. Let learners mention some action words they know.
2. Write simple sentences with adverbs on the board/ card.
3. Lead learners to read the sentences.
4. Help learners to recognise adverbs from a group of words.
5. Put learners in groups to write about four adverbs and form sentences with them.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Integrating Grammar in Written Language (Use of Postpositions)
content_standard: B3.5.5.1 Exhibit knowledge of understanding recognising and using postpositions.
indicator_description: Recognise postpositions in sentences.
examplars:
1. Let learners mention names of objects in the classroom and the environment.
2. Use postposition to form simple sentences on a card.
3. Lead learners to read the sentences aloud.
4. Let learners read the sentences in turns.
5. Encourage learners to recognise postpositions in sentences.
6. Let learners write the postpositions in their books.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Integrating Grammar in Written Language (Use of Postpositions)
content_standard: B3.5.5.1 Exhibit knowledge of understanding recognising and using postpositions.
indicator_description: Explore the use of postpositions such as above and below in sentences.
examplars:
1. Let learners mention names of objects in the classroom and the environment.
2. Use postposition in simple sentences.
3. Lead learners to explore the use of postpositions such as above and below in sentences.
4. Put learners into groups and let them form a given number of sentences using the postpositions “above” and “below.”
5. Let learners read aloud their sentences to the class and discuss the sentences with learners.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Integrating Grammar in Written Language (Use of Simple and Compound Sentences)
content_standard: B3.5.6.1 Exhibit knowledge of recognising features of compound sentence in writing.
indicator_description: Recognise conjunctions in compound sentences.
examplars:
1. Let learners mention names of objects in the classroom and the environment.
2. Use conjunctions to form simple sentences on the board.
3. Call learners to read aloud the sentences.
4. Lead learners to discuss and recognise conjunctions in the sentences.
5. Let learners form their own sentences using a chosen conjunction.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Integrating Grammar in Written Language (Use of Simple and Compound Sentences)
content_standard: B3.5.6.1 Exhibit knowledge of recognising features of compound sentence in writing.
indicator_description: Use conjunctions to form compound sentences from simple sentences.
examplars:
1. Let learners mention names of objects in the classroom and the environment.
2. Write compound sentences on the board and lead learners to read the sentences.
3. Lead learners to underline the conjunctions in the sentences.
4. Help learners to use conjunctions to form compound sentences from simple sentences.
5. Put learners into groups and let them form a given number of sentences using conjunctions.
6. Let learners read aloud their sentences to the class and discuss the sentences with learners.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Integrating Grammar in Written Language (Use of Simple and Compound Sentences)
content_standard: B3.5.6.1 Exhibit knowledge of recognising features of compound sentence in writing.
indicator_description: Use conjunctions such as but and because in forming compound sentences.
examplars:
1. Let learners mention names of objects in the classroom and the environment.
2. Write compound sentences on the board and lead learners to read the sentences.
3. Help learners to use conjunctions such as, “but” and “because” to form compound sentences.
4. Put learners into groups and let them form a given number of sentences using conjunctions “but” and “because.”
5. Let learners read aloud their sentences to the class and discuss the sentences with learners.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.5.7.1 Exhibit knowledge of recognising and spelling words correctly.
indicator_description: Write four and five letter words correctly.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Write four and five letter words on flashcards.
3. Show the cards to learners and lead them to mention the words.
4. Use the flashcards to encourage learners to write four and five letter words correctly in their books.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.5.7.1 Exhibit knowledge of recognising and spelling words correctly.
indicator_description: Write five and six letter words correctly.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Write five and six letter words on flashcards.
3. Show the cards to the learners and lead them to mention the words.
4. Use the flashcards to encourage learners to write five and six letter words correctly in their books.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.5.7.1 Exhibit knowledge of recognising and spelling words correctly.
indicator_description: Write more than five and six letter words correctly.
examplars:
1. Let learners say the letters of the alphabet.
2. Write five and six letter words on flashcards.
3. Show the cards to learners and lead them to mention the words.
4. Encourage learners write more five and six-letter words correctly in their books.
5. Let learners say the words to the class.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Building the Love and Culture of Reading
content_standard: B3.6.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge by reading short stories with correct intonation.
indicator_description: Read short stories aloud correctly.
examplars:
1. Let learners explore a popular rhyme they know.
2. Read a short story aloud and correctly to learners.
3. Discuss the short story with learners.
4. Call them out one by one to read short stories aloud correctly.
5. Ask them questions based on the story read.

subject: Ghanaian Language
content_standard: B3.6.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge by reading short stories with correct intonation.
indicator_description: Read short stories aloud correctly.
examplars:
1. Sing a popular song with learners.
2. Read a short story aloud and correctly to learners.
3. Discuss the story with learners and read the story again.
4. Call them out one by one to read short stories aloud correctly.
5. Call learners to read a sentence each from the story.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Reading Short Texts/Poems/Narratives/Short Stories and Respond to Them
content_standard: B3.6.3.1 Exhibit knowledge of reading passages with understanding.
indicator_description: Read stories with correct intonation.
examplars:
1. Sing a popular song with learners.
2. Read a story with correct intonation to the class.
3. Lead learners to learn how to read stories with correct intonation.
4. Call learners out one after the other to read the story.
5. Ask questions based on the story read for learners to answer.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Reading Short Texts/Poems/Narratives/Short Stories and Respond to Them
content_standard: B3.6.3.1 Exhibit knowledge of reading passages with understanding.
indicator_description: Say the title and author of the book read.
examplars:
1. Discuss with learners where to locate the title and author of a book.
2. Let learners bring out any reading book from the cupboard and lead them to mention the titles and authors of the books they have.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Reading Short Texts/Poems/Narratives/Short Stories and Respond to Them
content_standard: B3.6.3.1 Exhibit knowledge of reading passages with understanding.
indicator_description: Discuss and explain the meaning of some words and pictures in the passage.
examplars:
1. Let learners scan through the reading book and write down the words that are unfamiliar to them.
2. Put learners into groups to compare the words that they have written and come out with one list.
3. Let each group write their words on the board and lead them to pronounce the words as a group and individually.
4. Discuss the meaning of some of the words with learners and encourage them to find the meaning of the other words.

subject: Ghanaian Language
sub_strand: Reading Short Texts/Poems/Narratives/Short Stories and Respond to Them
content_standard: B3.6.3.1 Exhibit knowledge of reading passages with understanding.
indicator_description: Answer questions based on a passage read.
examplars:
1. Lead learners to read a passage from the class reader and let learners read the passage as a group and then individually.
2. Ask questions for learners to answer.
